item.title: RStudio is my new Python IDE
item.description: I was late testing Reticulate and how it harnesses Python in R, but I was not deceived. The fact that you can seamlessly work in python inside Rstudio amazed me. I have found some features Rstudio give who are really amazing. Here is the list. 1. The possibility to execute a chunk of python code with Ctrl + enter like with R As with R code when you have a Python script file, you can execute part of the code using RStudio shortcut like Ctrl + Enter.

item.title: Talks
item.description: My slides are mostly generated from R Markdown. You can access the .Rmd files by replacing the .html with .Rmd in the address bar of your browser. You can also by the same trick access the .pdf files. Anicet Ebou, 2020, Data Visualization and Transformation in R, a talk at satRday Abidjan 2020 (slides). Anicet Ebou, 2020, Data Analysis with R: Akwaba, slides. Les outils, slides. La visualisation des données, slides.

item.title: Tutorials
item.description: You will find tutorials on various subject around statistics, bioinformatics and R. Tutoriels en francais Anicet Ebou, 2020, Importer ces données dans R (slides).

item.title: R is overtaking Python in microbes analysis
item.description: Microbial analysis using amplicon genes is a good field for data analysis. The fact that microbes are ubiquitous in our environments and that high throughput sequencing causes a data deluge are the main causes. Crucial steps in microbial analysis are primers removing, demultiplexing, filtering and trimming of sequences, fitting of error models, sequences clustering and taxonomic assignment. Among above steps sequences clustering and taxonomic assignments appears as the most challenging. Sequences clustering, aims to reduce inherent PCR and DNA sequencing errors.

item.title: Why do we need to teach
item.description: Context It came to my mind to write this short post to put emphasis on the importance for people to share it. It became a need for me to ouline this point because of the reality that best mind are not always willing to share knowledge, sometimes because they don’t see the importance of teachnig. And this fact limit our true power and evolution as a community. Why It’s important to teach because teaching prove to yourself that you master the subject (at a certain level at least).

item.title: About
item.description: I’m a bioinformatician engineer. I’m doing a thesis in bioinformatics at the Doctoral school, National Polytechnic Institute of Yamoussoukro (Ivory Coast) advised by Dominique Koua. I have developped some R packages, such as afrods and tidygapminder. I begun Abidjan R users in 2016, and the community is growing rapidly. I initiated the satRday Abidjan conference in 2020. I love toxins (like spider toxins), pretty perl codes and coffee. I hate slow computer or connections.

item.title: How I Found Myself doing Data science in marketing
item.description: Since February 26 i am working at Seedstars Ivory Coast in a venture named Bora digital who works mostly in marketing and digital marketing. From a first background of agronomy (my Bsc) to bioinformatics (my master), at each step i have faced surprises, questions and oppositions from peoples around me. How do you did it? Why are you know working in marketings ? Why are you not as your peers working in farms or at least pursuing bioinformatics studies?

item.title: How to follow and engage with the R community
